rulings.Case Title: CORINNE WOODS et al VS. JOHN ARNTZ, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS DIRECTOR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 3, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST REBECCA EVANS' Motion For Reasonable Attorneys' Fees. Denied without prejudice. Given that no substantive decision has been made as to the validity of Proposition B, it would be premature for the Court to determine that Real Party in Interest is entitled to fees under CCP sec. 1021.5. For example, Real Party in Interest cannot establish at this stage of the litigation that a significant benefit has been conferred on the general public, as our Supreme Court has held that there is no value in putting an invalid measure on the ballot. (American Federation of Labor v. Eu (1984) 36 Cal.3d 687, 697.) Real Party in Interest may re-file her motion once a final determination has been made on the validity of Proposition B. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEVENS VS. JOHN MILLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff John Steven'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents- Set Two Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 13, Plaintiff John Steven'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To A Request For The Production Of Documents- Set Two Plaintiff John Stevens' Motion to Compel Further Responses to A Request for Production of Documents and for Sanctions is denied. Plaintiff Stevens' Document Request No. 20 seeking profit and loss statements related to the operation of the bar located at 3231 Fillmore Street, San Francisco, California for the time period the bar was owned by Defendants Touraj Boussina and Perry Boussina are completely irrelevant to the claims asserted by Plaintiff John Stevens and will not lead to the discovery of admissible evidence as the issue in the case is not the operation and or profitability of the bar located at 3231 Fillmore Street, San Francisco, California, but rather the failed business arrangement between Plaintiff John Stevens and his reliance upon John Miller to purchase the bar. The request for sanctions is denied as to each party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHELDON FONG VS. EAST WEST BANK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanction For Willful Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And To Continue Trial Date And Summary Judgment Motion Filing And Notice Deadlines
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions And For Monetary Sanction For Willful Failure To Comply With Discovery Order And To Continue Trial Date And Summary Judgment Motion Filing And Notice Deadlines. Pro Tem Judge Margaret M. Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant¿s motion for terminating sanctions or in the alternative, for dismissal of the complaint, is denied in light of plaintiff¿s service of amended responses to defendant¿s special interrogatories. Defendant¿s alternative motion for an order deeming admitted the requests for admission that were the subject of the discovery order entered on August 14, 2014, is denied without prejudice because the requests for admission that are a subject of that order were not included in defendant¿s motion. Defendant¿s motion for monetary sanctions is granted in part. The court orders plaintiff to pay sanctions in the amount of $1,300 to defendant within 10 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendant¿s motion to continue the trial date and summary judgment motion filing and notice deadlines must be directed to the Presiding Judge in accordance with Local Rule 6.0. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to margaret.niver@pillsburylaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
rulings.Judge: Judge Pro Tem: Margaret M. Niver

rulings.Case Title: FLYNN AND ENSLOW, INC. VS. DARREN WELTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANT DARREN WELTER'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion is denied in its entirety. The motion is denied as to issues 1-4. There is a triable issue of material fact whether plaintiff authorized defendant's conduct and whether defendant interfered with plaintiff's customer relationships. The motion is denied as to issues 5-8. The declaration of Susan Salamone shows that defendant purchased products/equipment on plaintiff's behalf, but plaintiff never received it. The trier of fact could infer the fraudulent intent based on defendant's conduct. The court rejects the statute of limitations argument. At par. 12 of the Salmone dec., plaintiff attaches invoices from 2011 and 2012 that show that plaintiff allegedly purchased some frames but they were never received. The motion is denied as to issue 9. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether the customer list qualifies as a trade secret. (Salamone dec., pars. 9-10). The motion is denied as to issue 10. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant sanctioned plaintiff's conduct and whether plaintiff is entitled to an accounting. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MURILLO VS. ORIGINAL JOE'S INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative , Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 9, DEFENDANT ORIGINAL JOE'S INC., JOE'S UNION L.P. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative , Summary Adjudication The court grants summary judgment in favor of Original Joe's, Inc. The declaration of Marie Duggan demonstrates that Original Joe's, Inc. does not have any role in the operation of the restaurant and it is not plaintiff's employer. Plaintiff fails to present any controverting evidence regarding that issue. The court denies plaintiff's request for a continuance per CCP 437c(h). The outstanding discovery is focused on finding a new class representative. The discovery is unrelated to the employer/employee issue. The court denies without prejudice the motion as to Joe's Union, L.P. The settlement does not comply with CRC 3.769 and 3.770 because the court never approved the settlement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CCP 437c(g) with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 11, PLAINTIFF JOY B Notice Ofmotion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And Production Of Documents Plaintiff Joy B's ("Joy") Motion to Compel Defendant's Further Responses to Request for Production of Documents is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The motion to compel production of documents under Requests Nos. 27 and 62 are granted as they relate directly to matters relevant to this lawsuit. The motion to compel production of documents under Request No. 28 is DENIED as excessively broad and may intrude in the privacy rights of third parties not party to this lawsuit. Since no party sought sanctions, no sanctions are awarded to any party. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Thruth Of Matters Specified In Pltf'S Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, December 10, 2014, Line 4: PLAINTIFF ABSOLUTE RESOLUTIONS V LLC'S Motion For Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Order That The Genuineness Of Documents And Truth Of Matters Specified In Plaintiff Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Margaret M. Niver, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff¿s unopposed motion to compel responses to form interrogatories, request for production of documents, for an order deeming admitted the genuineness of the documents and the truth of the matters specified in plaintiff¿s first set of requests for admissions and for sanctions is granted. Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objection, to plaintiff¿s first set of form interrogatories and request for production of documents, within 20 days of service of notice of entry of this order. The genuineness of the documents and the truth of the matters specified in plaintiff¿s first set of requests for admission are deemed admitted. Defendant is ordered to pay plaintiff sanctions in the sum of $290 within 20 days of service of notice of entry of this order.
